sources.full: Valda's Spire of Secrets: Player Pack 2

subclassFeature.name: Pistolero
entries: {@i Fire Six-Shooters with Incredible Speed}

Bullets are power, and you have long believed that more bullets equal more power. Your expertise is in delivering a hail of deadly fire to pulverize your enemies. Not every bullet needs to be accurate to make a difference.

subclassFeature.name: Close-Quarters Shooting
entries: Being within 5 feet of an enemy doesn't impose {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on your attack rolls with Ranged weapons.

subclassFeature.name: Fan the Hammer [Maneuver]
entries: When you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action with a Ranged weapon that doesn't have {@itemProperty 2H|XPHB|Two-Handed}, you can expend one Risk Die as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to make two additional ranged attacks with that weapon. These additional attacks always have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB}, regardless of circumstance. You can't use the {@itemMastery Automatic|ValdaGunslinger} mastery property with these attacks, and you must have a free hand to use this maneuver.

subclassFeature.name: Disarm
entries: When you score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} and trigger your {@classFeature Gut Shot|Gunslinger|ValdaGunslinger|5} feature against a creature, you can disarm the target instead of lodging a projectile in it. The target drops one object of your choice that it's holding, with the object landing in a space of your choice up to 15 feet away from the target.

subclassFeature.name: Showdown [Maneuver]
entries: When you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB}, you can expend one Risk Die to draw a Ranged weapon and make an attack using it. Add the Risk Die to the damage roll. On a hit, the target has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against creatures other than you on the first round of combat.

subclassFeature.name: Bullet Time
entries: Once on each of your turns when you make a ranged attack using a weapon, you can gain {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the roll.

subclassFeature.name: Heroic Sorcery
entries: {@i Wield Swords and Sorcery as a Reincarnated Hero}

You are the reincarnation of a legendary hero known to have slain many terrible foes, and your sorcerous magic draws from your legendary past. Being called back to life, whether by the gods, a very powerful mage, or a cycle of reincarnation, has awakened a well of magic and your ancient instincts for battle.

subclassFeature.name: Heroic Spells
entries: When you reach a Sorcerer level specified in the Heroic Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

entries.caption: Heroic Spells
rows: {@spell Arc Blade|ValdaPlayerPack2}, {@spell Burning Blade|ValdaPlayerPack2}, {@spell Frigid Blade|ValdaPlayerPack2}, {@spell Heroism|XPHB}, {@spell Magic Weapon|XPHB}, {@spell Mirror Image|XPHB}, {@spell Shield|XPHB}

subclassFeature.name: Martial Sorcery
entries: Your past heroic life grants you the following benefits.

entries.name: Heroic Soul
entries: At the start of each of your turns, you can spend 1 Sorcery Point to gain {@dice 1d6} plus your Sorcerer level in {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} (no action required).

entries.name: Innate Bladework
entries: Whenever you attack with a weapon with which you have proficiency while your {@classFeature Innate Sorcery|Sorcerer|XPHB|1} feature is active, you can use your Charisma modifier for the attack and damage rolls instead of using Strength or Dexterity.

entries.name: Martial Training
entries: You gain proficiency with Martial weapons and training with Light armor, Medium armor, and {@item Shield|XPHB|Shields}.

subclassFeature.name: Extra Attack
entries: You can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn. Moreover, you can cast one of your Sorcerer cantrips that has a casting time of an action in place of one of those attacks.

subclassFeature.name: Mystical Maneuvers
entries: You can use the following martial maneuvers.

entries.name: Blinding Attack
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack using a weapon or {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can expend two Sorcery Points as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to attempt to blind the target. Add {@damage 2d8} to the attack's damage roll. The target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC or have the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. At the end of each of its turns, the target repeats the save, ending the condition on itself on a success.

entries.name: Ruinous Blow
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack using a weapon or {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can expend two Sorcery Points as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to sunder its defenses. Add {@damage 2d8} to the attack's damage roll. The target has a -3 penalty to AC until the end of your next turn.

entries.name: Wounding Strike
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack using a weapon or {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can expend two Sorcery Points as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to deal the target a bleeding wound. Add {@dice 2d8} to the attack's damage roll. A creature with a bleeding wound can't regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} and takes Necrotic damage equal to {@dice 1d8} for each of its wounds at the start of each of its turns. This damage ignores {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} and {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB}. A creature can take an action to staunch all of its bleeding wounds.

subclassFeature.name: Heroic Haste
entries: Whenever you start casting {@spell Haste|XPHB} targeting yourself, you can modify it so that it doesn't require {@status Concentration|XPHB}. If you do so, when the spell ends, you don't have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition or a {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} of 0.

subclassFeature.name: Dragon Domain
entries: {@i Channel the Might of Dragon Gods}

While dragons are the foremost worshipers of the Dragon Domain, the draconic deities accept the worship of any who crave wealth or power. Clerics of the Dragon Domain, often considered cultists, can channel the power and aspect of dragons, unleashing fearsome elemental magic and manifesting the awe-inspiring presence of an ancient wyrm.
The principal gods of the Dragon Domain include Tiamat, the five-headed mother of chromatic dragons, and Bahamut, the just and wise god of metallic dragons.

subclassFeature.name: Chromatic Affinity
entries: Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, choose Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Poison. When you cast a Cleric spell or use a Cleric feature that deals Necrotic or Radiant damage, you can change that damage to the chosen type.

Once per turn when you deal damage of the chosen damage type to a creature, you can deal extra damage of that type equal to your Cleric level to that creature. You can deal this extra damage a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Dragon Domain Spells
entries: Your connection to this divine domain ensures you always have certain spells ready. When you reach a Cleric level specified in the Dragon Domain Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

entries.caption: Dragon Domain Spells
rows: {@spell Chromatic Orb|XPHB}, {@spell Command|XPHB}, {@spell Darkvision|XPHB}, {@spell Dragon's Breath|XPHB}

subclassFeature.name: Draconic Majesty
entries: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can present your {@item Holy Symbol|XPHB} and expend a use of your Channel Divinity to manifest an aura of draconic authority around yourself in a 30-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation}. Choose the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} or {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition. Each creature of your choice in the {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have the chosen condition for 1 minute. A creature {@condition Charmed|XPHB} or {@condition Frightened|XPHB} by this feature makes another Wisdom saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the condition on itself on a success.

subclassFeature.name: Wyrm's Blessing
entries: You can expend a use of your Channel Divinity to cast {@spell Dragon's Breath|XPHB} or {@spell Protection from Energy|XPHB} on yourself rather than expending a spell slot. When you cast either spell in this way, the spell doesn't require {@status Concentration|XPHB}. This spell ends early if you cast that spell again, have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition, or die.

subclassFeature.name: Legendary Aspect
entries: By conjuring the wings, claws, or tail of an ancient dragon, you can take one of the following Legendary Actions immediately after another creature's turn. Once you use this feature to take a Legendary Action, you can't take that one again until the start of your next turn. You can use this feature three times and regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore one use of it by expending a level 2+ spell slot (no action required).

entries.name: Rend
entries: You move up to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} and cast a Cleric cantrip that has a casting time of an action or make one melee attack using a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}. The attack uses your Wisdom for the attack and damage rolls instead of using Strength or Dexterity.

entries.name: Tail Swipe
entries: Each Large or smaller creature you choose within 10 feet of you gains the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.

entries.name: Wingbeat
entries: You immediately move up to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}. During this movement, you have a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} up to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} and don't provoke {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}.

subclassFeature.name: Beastborne
entries: {@i Channel Lycanthropic Fury}

Whether by infected bite or insidious curse, your blood was infected with the magic of lycanthropy. A ferocious, bestial form lies just beneath your skin, awaiting the allure of blood and the thrill of a hunt. Perhaps, if you can avoid the violence or the siren call of moonlight, you can resist the lycanthropic urge. Or perhaps you might embrace the infection as a blessing or wield it as a fearsome weapon.

subclassFeature.name: Lycanthrope
entries: Your true form is a bestial hybrid, granting you the following benefits.

entries.name: Claws
entries: You have a set of vicious claws that you can use to make {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB|Unarmed Strikes}. You can use your Dexterity modifier instead of your Strength modifier for the attack and damage rolls of your {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB|Unarmed Strikes}. When you deal damage with an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can deal Slashing damage equal to {@dice 1d6} plus your Strength or Dexterity modifier instead of the strike's normal damage.

entries.name: Climb Speed
entries: You gain a {@variantrule Climb Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You gain {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} with a range of 60 feet. If you already have {@sense Darkvision|XPHB}, its range increases by 60 feet.

subclassFeature.name: Bestial Aspect
entries: In the throes of combat, you gradually lose control and slip into a beastlike form. The degree of your transformation is represented by your Bestial Aspect Level, which starts at 0. Your Bestial Aspect Level grants you benefits, as shown on the Bestial Aspect table. You gain all the benefits of your Bestial Aspect Level and lower.

When you deal damage to an enemy, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to increase your Bestial Aspect Level by 1. If you don't deal damage to an enemy for 1 minute, your Bestial Aspect Level resets to 0.

entries.caption: Bestial Aspect
rows: {@b Carnage.} You gain a +2 bonus to your damage rolls with weapons and {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB|Unarmed Strikes}.
{@b Fast Movement.} Your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 10 feet.
{@b Blood Frenzy.} You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against any creature that doesn't have all its {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.
{@b Thick Hide.} If you aren't wielding a {@item Shield|XPHB}, you gain a +2 bonus to AC.

{@b Retaliation.} When you take damage from a creature that is within 5 feet of yourself, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to make one melee attack against that creature, using a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB|Unarmed Strike}.

subclassFeature.name: Feral Howl
entries: When you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB}, you can roll a {@dice d4} and increase your Bestial Aspect Level to the number rolled.

subclassFeature.name: Bloodthirsty Fury
entries: You have gained control over your bestial rage, granting you the following benefits.

entries.name: Bestial Mark
entries: You can cast {@spell Hunter's Mark|XPHB} as a part of the {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to increase your Bestial Aspect Level.

entries.name: Greater Carnage
entries: The damage bonus of your Carnage benefit increases to +3.

subclassFeature.name: Monstrous Resilience
entries: Once per turn when you take damage, you can reduce the total damage you take, to a minimum of 0. The reduction equals your Constitution modifier plus your Bestial Aspect Level.

subclassFeature.name: Circle of the City
entries: {@i Master the Urban Jungle}
The Circle of the City draws its power from buildings, streets, and people. These "Urban Druids" make their homes in sprawling settlements, far from the domains of their wild brethren.

An Urban Druid's magic can twist the city streets into knots and raise maze-like walls in alleyways. They might leap from rooftop to rooftop, commune with doors and walls for information, or transform into a rat to go unseen.

subclassFeature.name: Circle of the City Spells
entries: When you reach a Druid level specified in the Circle of the City Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

subclassFeature.name: City Shape
entries: You can expend a use of your {@classFeature Wild Shape|Druid|XPHB|2} to cast {@spell Meld into Stone|XPHB}, {@spell Passwall|XPHB}, or {@spell Stone Shape|XPHB} without a spell slot.

subclassFeature.name: Urban Druid
entries: Your magic and skills are specialized for city life, granting you the following benefits.

entries.name: Street Savvy
entries: Choose {@skill Acrobatics|XPHB}, {@skill History|XPHB}, {@skill Intimidation|XPHB}, {@skill Investigation|XPHB}, {@skill Persuasion|XPHB}, or {@skill Stealth|XPHB}. You gain a bonus to checks using this skill equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum bonus of +1). You can change your selection when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Urban Magic
entries: Your magic takes on the aesthetic trappings of the urban landscape. Spells like {@spell Entangle|XPHB}, {@spell Grasping Vine|XPHB}, {@spell Spike Growth|XPHB}, and {@spell Wall of Thorns|XPHB} might cause growths of cobblestones, twisting pipes, or broken glass. The spells {@spell Speak with Plants|XPHB}, {@spell Transport via Plants|XPHB}, and {@spell Tree Stride|XPHB} can target structures instead of plants.

subclassFeature.name: Object Shape
entries: When you assume a {@classFeature Wild Shape|Druid|XPHB|2} form, you can choose the form of an {@creature Animated Object|XPHB} created by the {@spell Animate Objects|XPHB} spell. You choose the object's size (up to Large). At Druid level 10, you can also shape-shift into a Huge object.

subclassFeature.name: Wall Warp
entries: When a creature you can see within 60 feet of you takes damage from an attack, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to cast {@spell Wall of Stone|XPHB} between the target and the attacker. When you cast the spell in this way, you create a single 10-foot-by-10-foot panel that is 1 inch thick (AC 15, 30 HP, and {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to Poison and Psychic damage), which disappears at the end of your next turn. The panel takes the damage of the attack and disappears early if it is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore your use of it by expending a level 3+ spell slot (no action required).

subclassFeature.name: Urban Colossus
entries: While shape-shifted into an {@creature Animated Object|XPHB}, you gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Armor Class
entries: Until you leave the form, your AC equals 18.

entries.name: Limited Damage Threshold
entries: Once per turn when you take less than 10 damage from a single attack or effect, you can reduce that damage to 0.

entries.name: Multiattack
entries: You can use Slam twice when you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action.

entries.name: Unstoppable
entries: You can move through the space of any creature. Moving through its space doesn't cost you extra movement, but you can't stop in the same space. If the creature is your size or smaller, it takes Bludgeoning damage equal to {@damage 2d6} plus your Wisdom modifier and has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition when you enter its space. A creature can take this damage once per turn.

subclassFeature.name: College of Masks
entries: {@i Embody Archetypal Characters Through Theater Masks}

Bards in the College of Masks are virtuoso performers who practice becoming the figures they portray, rather than merely imitating them. Bards who join in this college subscribe to the notion that all life is performed in a "Great Play," and that all people wear metaphorical masks at all times. To engage in this great stage drama, a masked Bard carries a number of Persona Masks, each bestowing the powers of a specific theatrical figure.

subclassFeature.name: Persona Masks
entries: You learn to create magical masks, each of which allows you to take on a different persona and gain some aspect of that archetypal character.

entries.name: Persona Masks
entries: You gain 3 masks of your choice from the "{@filter Persona Masks|optionalfeatures|feature type=vss:m}" section later in this subclass's description. You gain an additional mask at Bard level 6 (4 masks) and Bard level 14 (5 masks). You can exchange one of your masks for another one whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Wearing a Mask
entries: You can put on a mask or switch the mask you are wearing as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. Only you can gain the effects of your masks.

entries.name: Replacing a Mask
entries: If one of your masks is lost or stolen, you can remake the mask over the course of 8 hours, which can be done during a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Virtuoso Skill
entries: Once per turn when you make a {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB}, you can choose to make it with Charisma if the test doesn't use it already.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (a minimum of once) and regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Master of Many Faces
entries: You can wear two masks simultaneously, gaining the benefits of both.

subclassFeature.name: Magic Missile Mage
entries: {@i Cast Magic Missile Better Than Anyone}

{@spell Magic Missile|XPHB} is a staple wizard spell, beloved for its versatility and unerring accuracy. As a Missile Mage, however, your fondness for the spell borders on obsession. Your studies focus entirely on this spell, exploring every aspect of its casting, resulting in new augmentations and greater power.

subclassFeature.name: Magic Missile Savant
entries: Add the {@spell Magic Missile|XPHB} spell to your spellbook for free. Your expertise in this spell grants you the following benefits.

entries.name: Extra Darts
entries: When you cast {@spell Magic Missile|XPHB}, you create an extra dart of magical force. You create two extra darts at Wizard level 6, three extra darts at Wizard level 10, and four extra darts at Wizard level 14.

entries.name: Free Casting
entries: You can cast {@spell Magic Missile|XPHB} without a spell slot. You can do so a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once). You regain one expended use when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Penetrating Darts
entries: Your {@spell Magic Missile|XPHB} darts bypass any effect, such as the {@spell Shield|XPHB} spell, that specifically blocks the {@spell Magic Missile|XPHB} spell.

subclassFeature.name: Versatile Missiles
entries: You can accurately guide your {@spell Magic Missile|XPHB} darts to strike a target wherever you choose. When you cast {@spell Magic Missile|XPHB}, you can add one of the following Versatile Missile effects to a target struck by the darts. Each effect has a cost, which is the number of darts you must forgo to add the effect to the target. You can add different effects to different targets, paying the cost for each effect separately.

entries.name: Trip Missile (Cost: 1 Dart)
entries: The target must succeed on Strength saving throw against your spell save DC or have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.

entries.name: Blinding Missile (Cost: 3 Darts)
entries: The target must succeed on Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC or have the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} condition until the start of your next turn.

entries.name: Stunning Missile (Cost: 5 Darts)
entries: The target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC or have the {@condition Stunned|XPHB} condition until the start of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Shield of Missiles
entries: When you cast {@spell Magic Missile|XPHB}, you can direct the darts in a tight orbit around yourself, creating a protective 10-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from you, which lasts for up to 1 minute.

You gain a bonus to your {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} equal to the number of darts orbiting you (maximum bonus of +5). Whenever a creature within 120 feet misses you with an attack roll, one dart strikes the attacker, dealing damage as normal and decreasing the number of darts orbiting you by one.

Whenever the {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} enters the space of a creature you can see and whenever a creature you can see enters the {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} or ends its turn there, you can strike the target with any number of darts orbiting you, dealing damage as normal and decreasing the number of darts orbiting you. You can damage a creature with the {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} only once per turn.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore your use of it by expending a level 3+ spell slot (no action required).

subclassFeature.name: Giga-Missile
entries: When you cast {@spell Magic Missile|XPHB}, you can enhance the damage of the spell. You deal extra Force damage with each dart equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of 1).
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore your use of it by expending a level 6+ spell slot (no action required).

entries.name: Find Familiar
entries: You always have the {@spell Find Familiar|XPHB} spell prepared. Additionally, you can cast it as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action without expending a spell slot or Material components. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this spell (choose when you select this feat). Once you cast the spell using this benefit, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Expanded Options
entries: When you cast {@spell Find Familiar|XPHB}, you can choose one of the normal forms for your familiar or one of the following special forms: {@creature Imp|XMM}, {@creature Pseudodragon|XMM}, {@creature Quasit|XMM}, {@creature Sphinx of Wonder|XMM}, or {@creature Sprite|XMM}.

entries.name: Familiar Distraction
entries: When a creature within 5 feet of your familiar makes an attack roll, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to command your familiar to give the attack {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB}. You impose {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} in this way a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Upcast
entries: When you cast a spell, such as {@spell Magic Missile|XPHB}, that can be cast with a higher-level spell slot for more powerful effects, you can expend any additional spell slot to increase the spell's effective level by 1, to a maximum of level 9.

entries.name: Downcast
entries: When you cast a spell using a higher-level spell slot, you choose to recycle the excess magical energy instead of improving the spell. The spell is cast at its base level and you regain an expended level 1 spell slot.

entries.name: Magic Item Expert
entries: If one of your magic items calls for a saving throw, it uses the following DC if it is higher: 8 plus the modifier of the ability increased by this feat and your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}.

entries.name: Magic Item Recharge
entries: When you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, you can cause a magic item that regains charges or properties to recharge as if it were the next dawn. Once you use this trait, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Arcane Skill
entries: When you fail a {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB}, you can expend a spell slot to gain a bonus to the roll, potentially causing it to succeed. The bonus equals 2 plus the level of the spell slot expended.

entries.name: Vital Fuel
entries: When you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, you can expend a number of {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB} up to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} to recover expended spell slots. Choose spell slots to recover that have a combined level equal to no more than the number of {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB} expended. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Firestarter Magic
entries: You know the {@spell Fire Bolt|XPHB} cantrip and you always have the {@spell Burning Hands|XPHB} and {@spell Scorching Ray|XPHB} spells prepared. You can cast {@spell Burning Hands|XPHB} and {@spell Scorching Ray|XPHB} without expending a spell slot. Once you cast either spell in this way, you can't cast that spell in this way again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also cast these spells using spell slots you have of the appropriate level. The spellcasting ability for this feat's spells is the ability increased by this feat.

entries.name: Flare Damage
entries: Whenever you deal Fire damage and roll the highest number on any damage die, you can roll that damage die again and add it to the damage, rolling again if it is the highest number, and so on. You can add a maximum number of dice to the damage roll equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} (minimum of 1).

feat.name: Shock Trooper
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: First Strike
entries: When you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} and don't have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the roll, you can draw a weapon and make an attack using it.

entries.name: Rapid Advance
entries: During the first round of each combat, your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is doubled.

feat.name: Showman
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Expert Performer
entries: You gain proficiency in the {@skill Performance|XPHB} skill. If you already have proficiency in it, you gain {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB} in it.

entries.name: Taunt
entries: You can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to mock a creature within 15 feet of yourself. If the target can hear you (though it need not understand you), it has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the next attack roll it makes against a creature other than you before the end of its next turn. You can use this {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Blade Cantrips
entries: You learn two of the following cantrips of your choice: {@spell Arc Blade|ValdaPlayerPack2}, {@spell Burning Blade|ValdaPlayerPack2}, {@spell Frigid Blade|ValdaPlayerPack2}, or {@spell True Strike|XPHB}. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells (choose when you select this feat). Whenever you gain a new level, you can change your cantrip selection.

entries.name: Arcane Strike
entries: When you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn, you can replace one of the attacks with casting {@spell Arc Blade|ValdaPlayerPack2}, {@spell Burning Blade|ValdaPlayerPack2}, {@spell Frigid Blade|ValdaPlayerPack2}, or {@spell True Strike|XPHB}.

entries.name: Channeled Attack
entries: When you make an attack using Strength or Dexterity with a weapon with which you have proficiency, you can gain a bonus to the attack roll equal to your Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma modifier (minimum of +1). Choose the ability each time you use this benefit. You can gain this bonus a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 4}, reach 5 ft. {@h}4 ({@damage 1d4 + 2}) Bludgeoning damage.

trait.name: Adhesive (Object Form Only)
entries: The mock adheres to anything that touches it. The mock has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against any creature adhered to it. The target or a creature within 5 feet of it can detach the mock as an action.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 3}, reach 5 ft. {@h}3 ({@damage 1d4 + 1}) Piercing damage plus 2 ({@damage 1d4}) Acid damage.

bonus.name: Shape-Shift
entries: The mock shape-shifts to resemble a Tiny object while retaining its game statistics, or it returns to its true blob form. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying isn't transformed.

trait.name: Pack Tactics
entries: The pup has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against a creature if at least one of the pup's {@variantrule Ally|XPHB|Allies} is within 5 feet of the creature and the {@variantrule Ally|XPHB} doesn't have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 3}, reach 5 ft. {@h}3 ({@damage 1d4 + 1}) Piercing damage plus 2 ({@damage 1d4}) Cold damage.

monster.name: Yarn Golem
languages: understands Common plus one other language but can't speak

action.name: Poke
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 5}, reach 5 ft. {@h}8 ({@damage 2d4 + 3}) Piercing damage.

action.name: Tangle {@recharge}
entries: {@actSave dex} {@dc 12}, one creature that the golem can see within 5 feet. {@actSaveFail} If the target is Medium or smaller, it has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition (escape {@dc 12}). While {@condition Grappled|XPHB}, the target has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition.

trait.name: Mimicry
entries: The grep can mimic simple sounds it has heard, such as a whisper or chitter. A hearer can discern the sounds are imitations with a successful {@dc 10} Wisdom ({@skill Insight|XPHB}) check.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 6}, reach 5 ft. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d6 + 4}) Slashing damage.

trait.name: Incorporeal Movement
entries: The fright can move through other creatures and objects as if they were {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}. It takes 5 ({@damage 1d10}) Force damage if it ends its turn inside an object.

action.name: Life Drain
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 4}, reach 5 ft. {@h}9 ({@damage 2d8}) Necrotic damage. If the target is a creature, its {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum decreases by an amount equal to the damage taken.

trait.name: Just a Rock
entries: The rock counts as an object. Additionally, it can't take actions and its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} can't be increased.

monsterFluff.name: Flying Book
entries: Little can be said about the flying book that is not readily apparent. Though it is indistinguishable from an arcanist's spellbook while motionless, this animated arcane tome can flutter through the air at a moment's notice, using its cover as rudimentary wings. Flying books are playful, and enjoy the sensation of being written in.

monsterFluff.name: Grep
entries: A grep is an underground scavenger, known for using its keen vision, acute hearing, and silent flight to trick and steal from travelers, hoarding its possessions in well-hidden troves. Arcanists use greps as messengers and scouts, but they excel as retrievers; a grep can be given the name of an important object and sent to find it, returning a short time later with its prize.

monsterFluff.name: Mock
entries: The diminutive relative of the mimic, the mock is a tiny shapechanger that replicates small, valuable objects to lure in its victims. In the desert, a mock might take the form of a canteen of precious water, and in a dungeon, it may replicate a sizable jewel or a spare gold piece. If an adventurer is caught by surprise, a mock is just as dangerous as its larger relatives, for it can hide in far less conspicuous places.

monsterFluff.name: Pet Rock
entries: Widely considered to be the perfect familiar, requiring no food or water and possessing extremely high natural defenses, a pet rock can be conjured by most spellcasters. Though it does not act with any agility (indeed, it does not move at all), it can make a formidable weapon when thrown.

monsterFluff.name: Winter Wolf Pup
entries: When fully grown, a winter wolf is an apex predator of the snow, a terror of teeth and fur, stalking the tundra in packs. But before they develop their fearsome freezing breath, winter wolf pups are far more amicable (perhaps because they have yet to realize the scope of their own might), and will dutifully follow a master that feeds them well and treats them with respect.

monsterFluff.name: Fright
entries: Hauntings can usually be attributed to the usual spectral suspects: ghosts, specters, shadows, and poltergeists. But benign hauntings, where the restless spirit merely play tricks on the living, is usually caused by a fright. These spirits are usually the artifacts of children or entertainers, clinging to the afterlife for company and a semblance of affection, and are as such preoccupied with fun. They never willingly harm others when they haunt a house, and will serve any master that conjures them, if only for the sake of inclusion.

monsterFluff.name: Yarn Golem
entries: The greatest of golems are formed of iron, stone, and clay, but the least of them are constructed of yarn. Balled and sewn into a vaguely humanoid shape, yarn golems are among the first animated objects that arcanists learn to create, practicing with them obsessively until they master the basics and move onto more rigid materials. Yarn golems are friendly and playful, but risk foolishly unraveling themselves if they catch on sharp objects.

item.name: Gambler's Coin
entries: This Gold Piece has deep scratches on one side. It has 3 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn. When you make a {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB}, you can expend 1 charge to replace the {@dice d20} roll with a coin flip. On heads, the roll is treated as a 20 (though you don't score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} with an attack roll or activate any other effects which trigger on the roll of a 20). On tails, the roll is treated as a 1.

item.name: Leaden Manacles
entries: These magical {@item Manacles|XPHB} are used to restrain spellcasters. When a creature attempts to cast a spell or take the {@action Magic|XPHB} action while it is bound with these {@item Manacles|XPHB}, it makes a DC 20 ability check using its spellcasting ability. On a failure, the spell or {@action Magic|XPHB} action fails and the creature takes {@damage 4d6} Fire damage. The spell slot or use of that {@action Magic|XPHB} action is wasted, as well as the action, {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, or {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} taken.

item.name: Nolzur's Painted World
entries: This masterful painting, which is either Medium or Large, was crafted by a master painter using at least ten pots of {@item Nolzur's Marvelous Pigments|XDMG}. The painting depicts a serene painted world and acts as a portal to a demiplane in which that world exists. Creatures of the painting's size or smaller can pass through the portal into the demiplane.

Inside the painted world, a portal to the real world appears as a misty archway. If the face of the painting is blocked, the misty archway in the demiplane disappears. The location, objects, and creatures depicted within the painted world are determined by the painting's creator. Such objects and creatures dissipate into smoke if removed from the painting. Time passes at half speed within the painted world.

item.name: Reaper's Ammunition
entries: If a creature takes damage from this magic ammunition, it dies. The ammunition then becomes nonmagical.

item.name: Soul Figurine
entries: This smiling, human-shaped figurine contains a reserve of life force. If you drop to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} while attuned to the figurine, roll {@dice 2d10 + 10}. Your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} instead change to the number rolled. The figurine's expression becomes sorrowful and the figurine becomes nonmagical.

item.name: Bag of Cheer
entries: This large sack, which weighs 30 pounds and is made from red velvet, appears to be full of presents in colorful wrapping. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can pull a present from the bag and give it to another creature. The gift is tailored specifically to the recipient; the GM determines its nature or rolls on the Gifts table. The value of the gift can't exceed 100 GP.
Once three presents have been pulled from the bag, the bag can't be used again until the next dawn. Any additional presents removed from the bag are empty but well-decorated boxes.

item.name: Weapon Charms, Arrowhead
entries: A Weapon Charm is a small ornament fixed on a loop of string or chain. You can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to attach the charm to a weapon, usually on the weapon's pommel, or remove it from one. Attaching a charm causes the weapon to become a magic weapon that requires attunement. If the weapon is already magical, you must attune to it again to gain the charm's magical benefits, which are listed in the charm's description. A weapon can only have one attached charm at a time.
This golden charm depicts a stone arrowhead. While attached to a weapon, your ranged attacks using it ignore {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Half Cover} and {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Three-Quarters Cover}.

item.name: Weapon Charms, Die
entries: A Weapon Charm is a small ornament fixed on a loop of string or chain. You can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to attach the charm to a weapon, usually on the weapon's pommel, or remove it from one. Attaching a charm causes the weapon to become a magic weapon that requires attunement. If the weapon is already magical, you must attune to it again to gain the charm's magical benefits, which are listed in the charm's description. A weapon can only have one attached charm at a time.

This silver charm depicts a six-sided die. While attached to a weapon, it deals more potent critical hits. When you score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} with this weapon, if you roll the highest number on any damage die, you can roll another of that die and add it to the damage. You can add a maximum of 5 dice to the attack's damage roll in this way.

item.name: Weapon Charms, Flame
entries: A Weapon Charm is a small ornament fixed on a loop of string or chain. You can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to attach the charm to a weapon, usually on the weapon's pommel, or remove it from one. Attaching a charm causes the weapon to become a magic weapon that requires attunement. If the weapon is already magical, you must attune to it again to gain the charm's magical benefits, which are listed in the charm's description. A weapon can only have one attached charm at a time.

This brass charm resembles a burning fire. While attached to a weapon, it deals Fire damage instead of its normal damage type. When you hit a creature or object with this weapon, it begins {@hazard Burning|XPHB} for 1 minute. If you hit a burning target again with this weapon, the damage the burning target takes at the start of each of its turns increases by one step ({@dice d4} → {@dice d6} → {@dice d8} → {@dice d10} → {@dice d12}, to a maximum of {@dice 1d12}).

item.name: Weapon Charms, Hook
entries: A Weapon Charm is a small ornament fixed on a loop of string or chain. You can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to attach the charm to a weapon, usually on the weapon's pommel, or remove it from one. Attaching a charm causes the weapon to become a magic weapon that requires attunement. If the weapon is already magical, you must attune to it again to gain the charm's magical benefits, which are listed in the charm's description. A weapon can only have one attached charm at a time.

This bronze charm is shaped like a fishing hook. While attached to a weapon, if the weapon is on the same plane of existence as yourself, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to teleport it to your hand.

item.name: Weapon Charms, Lance
entries: A Weapon Charm is a small ornament fixed on a loop of string or chain. You can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to attach the charm to a weapon, usually on the weapon's pommel, or remove it from one. Attaching a charm causes the weapon to become a magic weapon that requires attunement. If the weapon is already magical, you must attune to it again to gain the charm's magical benefits, which are listed in the charm's description. A weapon can only have one attached charm at a time.

This copper charm depicts a short {@item lance|XPHB}. While attached to a weapon, once on each of your turns when you make a melee attack roll with this weapon against a creature you can see, you can lunge up to 15 feet toward your target before making the attack. This movement doesn't provoke {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}. You can perform this movement even if it causes you to travel through the air, though you fall after making the attack if you have nothing holding you aloft.

item.name: Weapon Charms, Lightning Bolt
entries: A Weapon Charm is a small ornament fixed on a loop of string or chain. You can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to attach the charm to a weapon, usually on the weapon's pommel, or remove it from one. Attaching a charm causes the weapon to become a magic weapon that requires attunement. If the weapon is already magical, you must attune to it again to gain the charm's magical benefits, which are listed in the charm's description. A weapon can only have one attached charm at a time.
This mithral charm depicts a wild lightning bolt. While attached to a weapon, it deals Lightning damage instead of its normal damage type and deals an extra {@damage 1d6} Lightning damage on a hit.

item.name: Weapon Charms, Quiver
entries: A Weapon Charm is a small ornament fixed on a loop of string or chain. You can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to attach the charm to a weapon, usually on the weapon's pommel, or remove it from one. Attaching a charm causes the weapon to become a magic weapon that requires attunement. If the weapon is already magical, you must attune to it again to gain the charm's magical benefits, which are listed in the charm's description. A weapon can only have one attached charm at a time.

This quartz charm resembles a quiver bristling with arrows. While attached to a weapon, it ignores the {@itemProperty LD|XPHB|Loading} property. {@itemProperty A|XPHB|Ammunition} you are carrying teleports into the weapon when needed.

item.name: Weapon Charms, +1 Blade
entries: A Weapon Charm is a small ornament fixed on a loop of string or chain. You can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to attach the charm to a weapon, usually on the weapon's pommel, or remove it from one. Attaching a charm causes the weapon to become a magic weapon that requires attunement. If the weapon is already magical, you must attune to it again to gain the charm's magical benefits, which are listed in the charm's description. A weapon can only have one attached charm at a time.

This adamantine charm resembles a miniature {@item Longsword|XPHB}. While attached to a weapon, you have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. If the weapon already gains such a bonus, like a {@item +2 Longsword|XDMG}, you choose which bonus to use; you can't use more than one.

item.name: Weapon Charms, +2 Blade
entries: A Weapon Charm is a small ornament fixed on a loop of string or chain. You can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to attach the charm to a weapon, usually on the weapon's pommel, or remove it from one. Attaching a charm causes the weapon to become a magic weapon that requires attunement. If the weapon is already magical, you must attune to it again to gain the charm's magical benefits, which are listed in the charm's description. A weapon can only have one attached charm at a time.

This adamantine charm resembles a miniature {@item Longsword|XPHB}. While attached to a weapon, you have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. If the weapon already gains such a bonus, like a {@item +2 Longsword|XDMG}, you choose which bonus to use; you can't use more than one.

item.name: Weapon Charms, +3 Blade
entries: A Weapon Charm is a small ornament fixed on a loop of string or chain. You can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to attach the charm to a weapon, usually on the weapon's pommel, or remove it from one. Attaching a charm causes the weapon to become a magic weapon that requires attunement. If the weapon is already magical, you must attune to it again to gain the charm's magical benefits, which are listed in the charm's description. A weapon can only have one attached charm at a time.

This adamantine charm resembles a miniature {@item Longsword|XPHB}. While attached to a weapon, you have a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. If the weapon already gains such a bonus, like a {@item +2 Longsword|XDMG}, you choose which bonus to use; you can't use more than one.

itemGroup.name: Weapon Charms
entries: A Weapon Charm is a small ornament fixed on a loop of string or chain. You can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to attach the charm to a weapon, usually on the weapon's pommel, or remove it from one. Attaching a charm causes the weapon to become a magic weapon that requires attunement. If the weapon is already magical, you must attune to it again to gain the charm's magical benefits, which are listed in the charm's description. A weapon can only have one attached charm at a time.

itemGroup.name: Weapon Charms, Blade
entries: A Weapon Charm is a small ornament fixed on a loop of string or chain. You can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to attach the charm to a weapon, usually on the weapon's pommel, or remove it from one. Attaching a charm causes the weapon to become a magic weapon that requires attunement. If the weapon is already magical, you must attune to it again to gain the charm's magical benefits, which are listed in the charm's description. A weapon can only have one attached charm at a time.

This adamantine charm resembles a miniature {@item Longsword|XPHB}. While attached to a weapon, you have a bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. The bonus is determined by the battery's rarity. If the weapon already gains such a bonus, like a {@item +2 Longsword|XDMG}, you choose which bonus to use; you can't use more than one.

optionalfeature.name: Angel
entries: This flawless alabaster mask depicts a serene Celestial. Once per turn when you deal damage to a creature with an attack or spell, you can expend a use of your {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard|XPHB|1} to deal extra Radiant damage to the target equal to one roll of your {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard|XPHB|1} die.

optionalfeature.name: Archmage
entries: This clay mask topped with a pointed hat bears the wrinkles of a cantankerous archmage. When you reach a Bard level specified in the following table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared while you wear this mask.

optionalfeature.name: Devil
entries: This obsidian mask bears the fangs and horns of a handsome fiend. When you take damage from a creature that is within 30 feet of yourself, you can expend a use of your {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard|XPHB|1} as a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to deal Fire damage to the creature. This damage equals two rolls of your {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard|XPHB|1} die, and you gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the damage dealt.

optionalfeature.name: Dragon
entries: When you put on this mask, and as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} on each of your turns while you wear it, you can expend a {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard|XPHB|1} die to exhale flames in a 15-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone}. Each creature in the {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone} makes a Dexterity saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, a creature takes Fire damage equal to two rolls of your {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard|XPHB|1} die, or half as much damage on a successful save.

optionalfeature.name: Faceless
entries: Two perfectly round eyeholes stare out of this otherwise featureless porcelain mask. While you wear this mask, you always have the {@spell Disguise Self|XPHB} spell prepared. Whenever you start casting the spell, you can modify it so that it doesn't require a spell slot. If you do so, the spell's casting time becomes 1 minute for that casting and its duration becomes 8 hours. The spell ends early if you remove this mask.

optionalfeature.name: Gladiator
entries: This grim metal helm, pockmarked and scratched, conceals the entire face. While you wear this mask, you have proficiency with Martial weapons and training with {@item Shield|XPHB|Shields}. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend a use of your {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard|XPHB|1} to make one attack with a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}.

optionalfeature.name: Hierophant
entries: In lieu of a face, this bronze mask bears the image of a holy symbol, with the barest of slits to see through. When you reach a Bard level specified in the following table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared while you wear this mask.

entries.caption: Hierophant Persona Spells
rows: {@spell Aid|XPHB}, {@spell Cure Wounds|XPHB}, {@spell Spare the Dying|XPHB}

optionalfeature.name: Jester
entries: This cloth mask of a grinning jester is adorned with bells and checkerboard patterns. When you put on this mask, and as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} on each of your turns while you wear it, you can move up to half your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} without provoking {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}. You can also expend a use of {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard|XPHB|1} to cast {@spell Vicious Mockery|XPHB} as a part of this mask's {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.

optionalfeature.name: Noble
entries: This gleaming silver mask has two rubies set into its eyesockets. You can use your {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard|XPHB|1} as a part of the {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to put on this mask. When a creature with your {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard|XPHB|1} die fails a {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB} while you wear this mask, it can roll the {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard|XPHB|1} die twice and add the higher result to the d20.

spell.name: Arc Blade

m.text: a Melee weapon with which you have proficiency and that is worth 1+ CP

entries: As lightning surges through the weapon used in the spell's casting, you make one attack. This attack can be a melee attack or ranged spell attack using the weapon with a range of 15 feet, as a wave of energy extends from the weapon. The attack uses your spellcasting ability for the attack and damage rolls instead of using Strength or Dexterity. If it is a ranged attack, it deals Lightning damage. Otherwise, it can deal Lightning damage or the weapon's normal damage type (your choice).

entriesHigherLevel.name: Cantrip Upgrade
entries: Whether you deal Lightning damage or the weapon's normal damage type, the attack deals extra Lightning damage when you reach levels 5 ({@dice 1d6}), 11 ({@dice 2d6}), and 17 ({@dice 3d6}).

spell.name: Burning Blade

m.text: a Melee weapon with which you have proficiency and that is worth 1+ CP

entries: Encasing the weapon used in the spell's casting in flames, you make one melee attack. The attack uses your spellcasting ability for the attack and damage rolls instead of using Strength or Dexterity. If the attack deals damage, it can be Fire damage or the weapon's normal damage type (your choice). If the weapon's damage die or any of the spell's damage dice roll the highest number, you can roll that die again and add it to the damage, rolling again if it is the highest number, and so on. You can add a maximum number of dice to the damage roll equal to your spellcasting ability modifier.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Cantrip Upgrade
entries: Whether you deal Fire damage or the weapon's normal damage type, the attack deals extra Fire damage when you reach levels 5 ({@dice 1d6}), 11 ({@dice 2d6}), and 17 ({@dice 3d6}).

spell.name: Frigid Blade

m.text: a Melee weapon with which you have proficiency and that is worth 1+ CP

entries: Encasing the weapon used in the spell's casting in ice, you make one melee attack. The attack uses your spellcasting ability for the attack and damage rolls instead of using Strength or Dexterity. The weapon's damage die increases by 1 step ({@dice d4} → {@dice d6} → {@dice d8} → {@dice d10} → {@dice d12} or {@dice 2d6}, your choice). The weapon's damage dice don't increase if it has more than one weapon damage die. If the attack deals damage, it can be Cold damage or the weapon's normal damage type (your choice).

entriesHigherLevel.name: Cantrip Upgrade
entries: Whether you deal Cold damage or the weapon's normal damage type, the attack deals extra Cold damage when you reach levels 5 ({@dice 1d6}), 11 ({@dice 2d6}), and 17 ({@dice 3d6}).

spell.name: Candy Blast
entries: You summon a handful of hard candy—boiled sweets, mints, jelly beans, etc.—and hurl them at a creature you can see within range. Make a ranged spell attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 1d8} Force damage and the candy forms a pile on the ground in its space, causing a 5-foot-square area of your choice in the target's space to become {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}. The candy produced by this spell is edible but has no nutritional value, and vanishes after 1 minute, ending any {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Cantrip Upgrade
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d8} when you reach levels 5 ({@dice 2d8}), 11 ({@dice 3d8}), and 17 ({@dice 4d8}).

spell.name: Cheat
entries: You twist your fingers and fate seems to follow suit. Until the end of your next turn, you can reroll any ability check you make to play nonmagical games of skill and must use the new roll. Therefore, this spell could influence a game of poker, but not the result of a {@item Deck of Many Things|XDMG}.

spell.name: Flawed Reconstruction
entries: You stitch together the wounds of a creature you touch, which regains {@dice 3d6} {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. However, the mending is imperfect, and the target's {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum is reduced by {@dice 1d6}, to a minimum of 1.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The healing increases by {@dice 2d6} and the reduction increases by {@dice 1d6} for each spell slot level above 1.

spell.name: Transient Bulwark

m.text: a pearl worth 10+ GP, which the spell consumes

entries: A fragile, invisible shield protects you for the duration. The next attack roll against you has a -10 penalty to hit, and the spell ends.

spell.name: Zephyr's Feather
entries: You wave your hand and four alabaster feathers, magically sharpened to a razor's edge, appear and orbit around you until the spell ends. When you cast this spell and as {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} on your later turns, you can expend one of the feathers and make a ranged spell attack using it against a creature you can see within 120 feet of yourself. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 1d8} Force damage.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d8} for each spell slot level above 1.

spell.name: Blunder

time.condition: which you take when a creature you can see within range hits a target with an attack roll

entries: The attacker must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or suffer one of the following effects of your choice:

entries.name: Fumble
entries: The target drops one object of your choice that it's holding, with the object landing in a space you choose up to 10 feet away from the target. If the target drops the weapon it is using for the attack roll, it can make an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} for the triggering attack.

entries.name: Prat Fall
entries: The target has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition. The {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} from the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition applies against the triggering attack, potentially causing it to miss.

spell.name: Protect Threshold

components.m: an ounce of salt for each foot of the warded portal's perimeter

entries: Tracing arcane sigils along its boundary, you can ward a doorway, window, or other portal from entry. For the duration, an {@condition Invisible|XPHB} eldritch creature stalks the warded portal. Any creature that attempts to pass through the portal makes a Wisdom saving throw, taking {@damage 4d6} Psychic damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful save.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d6} for each spell slot level above 2.

spell.name: Stone Bones
entries: Choose a creature you can see within range. The target has {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage until the spell ends at the end of your next turn.

spell.name: Swift Flight
entries: Streaks of energy flow from the back of a creature you touch, tracing the shapes of wings. The target has a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of 60 feet and can hover until the end of its next turn. When the spell ends, the target falls if it is in the air and nothing is holding it aloft.

spell.name: Cosmic Horror
entries: For the briefest moment, you open a portal to an eldritch dimension in a 10-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} centered on a point you can see within range. Each creature within the {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} makes a Wisdom saving throw as it beholds the nightmarish dimension. On a failed save, a creature takes {@damage 6d6} Psychic damage and has the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition for the duration. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage only.
A creature {@condition Frightened|XPHB} by this spell makes another Wisdom saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d6} for each spell slot level above 3.

spell.name: Flashback
entries: Casting your mind through time, you make a subtle alteration to history, allowing you to make preparations for the current situation within the last 24 hours. Describe to your GM how the precautions you took ahead of time to affect the current situation. For example, you might have packed a particular piece of gear or arranged for a distraction to occur at this exact moment. The GM might call for an ability check if the preparations are sufficiently challenging; on a failure, the spell fails and has no effect.

A Flashback's effects are limited, as reality reasserts itself with the fewest possible changes to accommodate the alteration in history. The spell can't remove creatures or objects from an encounter, harm a creature, or cause creatures and objects that aren't being worn or carried by you to move. It can generally add an element to an encounter, but can't generally subtract them. The GM decides on the extent of the Flashback's effect.

spell.name: Rusting Grasp
entries: You conjure a potent acid at your fingertips, which can corrode metal and burn flesh. Make a melee spell attack against a creature or object within your reach. On a hit, a creature takes {@damage 6d4} Acid damage and it has a -3 penalty to its AC, to a minimum of 10, for 1 hour.

If you target a nonmagical metal object with this spell that isn't being worn or carried, you corrode and destroy parts of the object you choose that can fit within a 1-foot {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cube}.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 2d4} for each spell slot level above 3. If you target a nonmagical object, you can destroy an additional cubic foot for each slot level above 3.

spell.name: Séance

m.text: a crystal ball, deck of tarot cards, or ouija board, and incense worth 50+ GP

entries: You and three or more willing creatures lock hands to conjure a spirit from the afterlife to answer your questions. Describe or name a creature that is familiar to you. If the creature's soul is free and willing, it manifests as a ghostly specter. This spell fails if the spirit was the target of this spell within the last 10 days.

Until the spell ends, you can ask up to three questions of the specter. The specter knows only what it knew in life, including the languages it knew. Answers are usually brief, cryptic, or repetitive, and the specter is under no compulsion to offer a truthful answer if you are hostile to it or it recognizes you as an enemy. There is a {@chance 5} chance that this spell contacts the wrong spirit, one which will answer questions untruthfully or ambiguously.

spell.name: Mandy's Marvelous Dress
entries: A willing creature you touch is instantly clothed in a fabulous ball gown, perfectly tailored and complete with a set of glittering jewelry. You can choose for the dress to sit atop the creature's clothing or armor, or for it to magically replace its outfit. The creature can't remove the dress until the spell ends; similarly, the jewelry vanishes if it is removed.

entries.name: Elegant Flair
entries: Until the spell ends, when the wearer makes a {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB} using Charisma, it can replace the number it rolls with a 10.

entries.name: Glamorous Ward
entries: If the wearer of the dress is attacked by a creature that can see it, the attacker must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or miss with the attack. On a success, the attacker is immune to this effect until the spell ends.

entries.name: Duration
entries: This spell's duration varies depending upon when and where it was cast. If cast in a location where time passes normally, the spell lasts until the twelfth stroke of the next midnight, however long that may be. Elsewhere, the spell lasts for 24 hours. When the spell ends, the dress vanishes in a puff of sparkles, and the creature becomes clothed in its original outfit.

spell.name: Soul Effigy
entries: A Humanoid you touch makes a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a fragment of its soul is bound to the doll used in this spell's casting, which becomes an effigy of that creature. The spell ends if the effigy is destroyed or if the target is on another plane of existence. For the duration, you can use a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to do one of the following.

entries.name: Control
entries: You directly control the target's actions and movement on its next turn by manipulating the effigy's limbs. That target can only take the {@action Dash|XPHB}, {@action Disengage|XPHB}, {@action Hide|XPHB}, or {@action Utilize|XPHB} action. While controlled, the target's movements are jerky and unnatural.

entries.name: Harm
entries: You prod the effigy and deal {@damage 2d8} damage to the target. This damage is Bludgeoning, Piercing, Slashing, or another damage type, appropriate to the means by which you harm the effigy. This damage ignores {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} and {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB}.

entries.name: Restrain
entries: You submerge the effigy in water, causing the target to believe it is suffocating. The target has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition until the end of your next turn.

spell.name: Sword of Judgment
entries: This spell designates an area of protection in a 20-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} centered on a point you choose within range. Within that {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere}, a spectral sword appears over each creature, following the creature as long as it remains within the {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere}.

Whenever a creature within the {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} makes an attack or casts a spell, you can force that creature to make a Dexterity saving throw, as the sword above it falls. Resolve the Dexterity saving throw before the triggering attack or spell. On a failed save, the target takes {@damage 4d8} Force damage, or half as much damage on a successful save. A creature makes this save only once per turn.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d8} for each spell slot level above 5.

spell.name: Frenzy
entries: With a gesture, you reduce a crowd to base instinct and violence. Each creature in a 20-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} centered on a point you choose within range makes a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, a creature has the Frenzied condition for the duration.

A creature with the Frenzied condition loses the ability to distinguish between friend and foe, regarding all creatures it can see as enemies. While Frenzied, the creature chooses the targets for its attacks, spells, and abilities randomly from among the creatures it can see within range, and it must make an {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB} if any creature provokes one.
At the end of its turn, an affected creature can repeat its saving throw, ending the condition on itself early on a success.

spell.name: Rocks Fall
entries: Boulders appear in the air and fall in a 60-foot-radius, 120-foot-high {@variantrule Cylinder [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cylinder} centered on a point within range. Each creature in the {@variantrule Cylinder [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cylinder} makes a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes {@damage 10d8} Bludgeoning damage and is buried under the rubble. On a successful save, a target takes half as much damage only.

A buried creature has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} and {@condition Restrained|XPHB} conditions, and can take an action to make a Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) check against your spell save DC, freeing itself from the boulders and ending the {@condition Prone|XPHB} and {@condition Restrained|XPHB} conditions on itself on a success.

book.name: Valda's Spire of Secrets: Player Pack 2
book.author: Mage Hand Press

data.name: Subclasses
entries: At level 3, every class offers a character-defining choice called a subclass that grants you features at certain levels. Subclasses change how the class plays and further differentiate each class into a spectrum of discrete archetypes. For example, all Druids are channel the power of nature, but only the Circle of the City draws their power from the urban landscape.
This section offers seven new subclasses for the Gunslinger, Bard, Cleric, Druid, Ranger, Sorcerer, and Wizard.

data.name: Feats
entries: This chapter offers a collection of feats, which are special features not tied to a character class. A feat represents a talent or an area of expertise that gives a character special capabilities. It embodies training, experience, and abilities beyond what a class provides.
By whatever means you acquire a feat, you can take it only once unless its description says otherwise.

entries.name: General Feats
entries: These feats are in the General category.

data.name: Spells
entries: Without a repertoire of spells, a wizard is merely an academic with an overlarge hat. This section presents a brief compendium of new spells ranging from indispensable utility spells, to whimsical transmutations, to time-bending chronomancy.

data.name: Magic Items
entries: The magic items are presented in alphabetical order.

data.name: Familiars
entries: While many spellcasters opt to summon inconspicuous familiars, like cats, frogs, and owls, others use Find Familiar to bring forth creatures from the farthest reaches of the multiverse or bestow sentience on an inanimate object. This chapter introduces new familiar options.

When a character casts {@spell Find Familiar|XPHB}, they can choose a {@creature Flying Book|ValdaPlayerPack2}, {@creature Mock|ValdaPlayerPack2}, {@creature Pet Rock|ValdaPlayerPack2}, or {@creature Winter Wolf Pup|ValdaPlayerPack2} in addition to the spell's normal forms. If the character can choose the {@creature Imp|XMM}, {@creature Pseudodragon|XMM}, or {@creature Sprite|XMM} as familiar options, they can also choose the {@creature Fright|ValdaPlayerPack2}, {@creature Grep|ValdaPlayerPack2}, or {@creature Yarn Golem|ValdaPlayerPack2}.

entries.name: ON THE COVER
entries: Agustín Marceillac depicts a party of adventurers taking their first tentative steps into Valda's Spire of Secrets! Within the grand entry hall of the multidimensional spire, the lich has laid out row upon row of petrified adventurers—trophies and threats in equal measure.

No portion of this work other than the material designated as Open Game Content may be reproduced in any form without written permission.

{@i Disclaimer: Valda is completely unrelated to that other lich with godlike power. For starters, he'd never let a vampire cut off his hand or gouge out an eye. And if he did, he wouldn't let adventurers play around with the bits afterward.}
